steps.prompt: Which word is the main clause verb?

hints.Nullum: 'Nullum' is an adjective meaning 'no / not any'
hints.possit: 'possit' is subjunctive in the relative clause — not the main verb

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.Accusative: Neuter nominative and accusative look the same, but 'periculum' is the subject of 'est'

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'periculum, -i' is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension neuters have varied endings — 'periculum, -i' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have gen. in -us — 'periculum, -i' is 2nd declension

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'potest' — 'possit' is subjunctive
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'posset' — 'possit' is present
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'potuerit' — 'possit' is present subjunctive

steps.prompt: Why is the subjunctive used with 'possit'?
steps.target: Relative clause of characteristic — describing a type of danger
choices: Relative clause of characteristic — describing a type of danger

hints.Purpose clause: Purpose uses 'ut/ne' — this is a relative clause (introduced by 'quod') with subjunctive
hints.Result clause: Result uses 'ut' — 'quod' here is a relative pronoun with characteristic subjunctive
hints.Indirect statement: Indirect statement uses accusative + infinitive — this is a relative clause

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place) — don't confuse 'posui' with 'potui'
hints.potior, potiri, potitus sum: These are the parts of 'potiri' (to gain possession of)
hints.polliceor, polliceri, pollicitus sum: These are the parts of 'polliceri' (to promise)

hints.to be called: 'to be called' is 'appellari' — 'depelli' is the passive of 'depellere' (to drive away)
hints.to be sent: 'to be sent' is 'mitti' — 'depelli' means 'to be driven off'
hints.to be destroyed: 'to be destroyed' is 'deleri' — 'depelli' means 'to be repelled'

hints.pello, pellere, pepuli, pulsum: These are the parts of 'pellere' (to drive) — the base verb without the de- prefix
hints.appello, appellare, appellavi, appellatu: These are the parts of 'appellare' (to call/name) — a different verb
hints.compello, compellere, compuli, compulsum: These are the parts of 'compellere' (to compel) — a different compound

translation: There is no danger which cannot be repelled by me.
